explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A Digiday article references a CTO's role and a steering committee for generative AI governance. Additionally, a press release implies a commitment to understanding AI's impact through the release of research on AI-generated content and its brand impact.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This Digiday article on Omnicom's AI plan provides evidence for **governance** and **transparency**. It supports transparency by describing the capabilities of their AI agents and how clients can utilize them for various purposes like ideation and personalization. The mention of a CTO's role and a steering committee for generative AI governance further indicates support for the governance pillar.
relevant_sources.title: Omnicom Deepens AI Plan (Digiday)

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by implying a commitment to understanding AI's impact through the release of research on AI-generated content and its brand impact. Transparency is supported by mentioning an AI-driven platform and its capabilities, as well as the company's participation in the C2PA to standardize content verification and protect the authenticity of AI assets.
relevant_sources.title: With Omni Assist, Omnicom Leverages First-Mover Access to Open AI to Accelerate the Timeline from Insights to Outcomes

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: The company's 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report indicates that its internal use of Generative AI is governed by global Omnicom frameworks. Published materials describe the capabilities of the company's AI agents and an AI-driven platform, outlining how clients can utilize them for various purposes. The company also participates in the C2PA to standardize content verification and protect the authenticity of AI assets.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: The 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. This report indicates that the company's internal use of Generative AI is governed by global Omnicom frameworks, demonstrating transparency about the existence and purpose of their AI adoption.
relevant_sources.title: 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report

relevant_sources.summary: This Digiday article on Omnicom's AI plan provides evidence for **governance** and **transparency**. It supports transparency by describing the capabilities of their AI agents and how clients can utilize them for various purposes like ideation and personalization. The mention of a CTO's role and a steering committee for generative AI governance further indicates support for the governance pillar.
relevant_sources.title: Omnicom Deepens AI Plan (Digiday)

relevant_sources.summary: This press release provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. It supports governance by implying a commitment to understanding AI's impact through the release of research on AI-generated content and its brand impact. Transparency is supported by mentioning an AI-driven platform and its capabilities, as well as the company's participation in the C2PA to standardize content verification and protect the authenticity of AI assets.
relevant_sources.title: With Omni Assist, Omnicom Leverages First-Mover Access to Open AI to Accelerate the Timeline from Insights to Outcomes

summary.overall_findings: Omnicom's internal use of Generative AI is governed by global Omnicom frameworks, as indicated in its 2024 Corporate Responsibility Report. Additionally, published materials reference a CTO's role and a steering committee for generative AI governance, addressing two of the seven evaluated responsible AI pillars. Drawing from 13 publicly available sources, no qualifying public evidence was found for the majority of the 7 evaluated pillars.
